qa.overall_assessment: The BA analysis correctly identifies the root cause, target value, and primary affected file. However, the scope analysis is incomplete: BA declares 'No change to any other file' in the Won't Have section, but two additional active files (hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh and commands/push.md) will be broken or factually wrong after the change. The regression test at test_final_sweep.sh:64 and the Python AST assertion at line 73 both hard-assert the value 180 and will FAIL after the constant is changed to 600. commands/push.md is an operational slash-command spec (not passive documentation) and its TTL section will diverge from the real value. BA must be revised to include these files in scope before dev proceeds.

evidence_quality.rationale: BA directly read agents/push-analyst.md and confirmed PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 at line 164 by Read tool. Location is confirmed by QA independent grep. Root cause claim (round-trip exceeds 180s) is corroborated by execute-push.py line 166 which aborts on expired grants, and the prior task history (120->180 increase). Target value 600 is user-verbatim. Evidence quality for what BA did claim is adequate.

scope_alignment.rationale: User's verbatim requirement states 'Increase PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS to 600'. BA narrowed scope to 'agents/push-analyst.md only' and placed all other files in Won't Have. However, the constant is a writer site, and two reader/verifier sites will break: (1) hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh AC4 assertions hard-assert the value 180; (2) commands/push.md contains the operational specification that documents 180 as the TTL. Silently failing these reader sites is a scope_alignment gap — BA's Must Have and Won't Have do not account for the files that depend on the constant's value.

investigation_completeness.rationale: BA performed a grep for the constant name and found only agents/push-analyst.md:164. However, BA did not check for numeric-value references (grep for '180' in test files) or check what test assertions exist that validate the current value. The scope_expansion field only tracked the constant NAME, not assertions referencing the numeric value 180. A complete investigation would have discovered hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh lines 64 and 73 which assert val.value == 180 via Python AST.

affected_file_accuracy.rationale: BA's affected_files list = ['agents/push-analyst.md']. QA independently verified three files are functionally affected: agents/push-analyst.md (correct), hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh (missing — contains regression assertions that will fail), and commands/push.md (missing — operational spec that documents the 180s value and will diverge). All three file paths exist and contain the relevant content as described.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.rationale: AC1 and AC2 in acceptance-criteria-dev-20260527-063758-T3.json are well-formed GIVEN/WHEN/THEN with executable check methods (grep pattern-match and git_diff_file_list). No drift detected between the AC text and the described verification recipe for what BA did specify. Note: the two ACs are insufficient in scope (see scope_alignment), but the AC text itself is not drifted from what BA intends to verify.

root_cause_verification.rationale: QA independently confirmed PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 at agents/push-analyst.md:164 via grep. The expiry check at scripts/execute-push.py:166 (`if expires_at <= datetime.now(timezone.utc): return 1`) directly confirms that an expired grant aborts the push. The round-trip path (orchestrator -> push-analyst subagent -> orchestrator result-processing -> execute-push.py grant read) is correctly described. Prior TTL history (120->180 in task 20260519-211515 R4/AC4) is consistent with the BA narrative.

target_value_verification.rationale: User requirement states 'Increase PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS to 600 (10 minutes)'. No derivation needed; the value is prescribed directly. 600s = 10 minutes is a reasonable upper bound for the described round trip.

security_assessment.ttl_increase_impact: The increase from 180s to 600s extends the bounded validity window. For grants that are consumed atomically (the normal approved/warn path), there is no replay risk because the grant file is unlinked before the sentinel is written. However, on non-consumed paths (HEAD drift at execute-push.py:246, blocked verdict at line 215), grants remain valid until expiry. A longer TTL widens the replay window on these preserved-grant paths from 3 minutes to 10 minutes. This is an accepted tradeoff, not a regression, because: (1) preserved grants still require matching nonce, session_id, branch, remote_name, and head_sha; (2) the user explicitly specified 600s knowing this tradeoff.
security_assessment.remote_url_validation_gap: OBSERVATION_ONLY (pre-existing): execute-push.py validates remote_name but not remote_url. A same-name remote URL change within the grant validity window is not caught by Chain-B validation. This gap exists at 180s and at 600s. It is pre-existing and out of scope for this task.
existing_controls_unchanged: single-use unlink on normal consumption path

failures.finding: BA's Won't Have declares 'No change to any other file'. But test_final_sweep.sh:64 greps for PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 exactly, and line 73 contains Python AST assertion `assert isinstance(val, ast.Constant) and val.value == 180`. After changing the constant to 600, these two assertions will FAIL, causing the AC4 test suite regression check to report AC4 V1 FAIL and AC4 V2 FAIL. This is a direct blocker: the regression test that was previously tracking the correct TTL value will start reporting failures.
failures.evidence: hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh line 64: grep -qE '^PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*180[[:space:]]*$' agents/push-analyst.md; line 73: assert isinstance(val, ast.Constant) and val.value == 180
failures.recommendation: BA must add hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh to the affected files list and update the AC4 V1 and V2 assertions to check for 600, not 180. AC4 V3 (line 86) uses a bare PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS name match which will still pass after the change, but should be updated to also require the 600 value to prevent the test from becoming a no-op guard.

failures.finding: commands/push.md is not passive documentation — it is the operational specification for the /push slash command. Lines 216-228 explicitly state 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 seconds' and document the rationale for the prior 120->180 increase. After the constant changes to 600, this section will be factually incorrect and diverge from the real runtime value. BA did not include this file in scope.
failures.evidence: commands/push.md:218-219: 'The push-analyst writes its Chain-B grant with a default TTL of PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 seconds'. QA grep confirmed this text exists at lines 216-228.
failures.recommendation: BA must add commands/push.md to the affected files list (Must Have or Should Have). The TTL section must be updated to document 600s, revise the history to note the 180->600 increase for this task, and retain the distinction from write-commit-grant.py.
failures.note: This is a major finding because it creates command-spec divergence. Dev could proceed with only the agents/push-analyst.md change and have runtime-correct behavior, but the operational spec would be stale. Classified major rather than critical because runtime behavior is not affected.

failures.finding: BA's scope_expansion search only tracked the CONSTANT NAME 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS'. BA did not search for the numeric value 180 in test assertions, which is the pattern that would reveal that test_final_sweep.sh contains assertions against the literal number 180. A complete investigation would have grep'd for '180' in test files or checked what tests validate the TTL value.
failures.evidence: BA scope_expansion.additional_found = [] and all_occurrences = ['agents/push-analyst.md:164']. QA grep for '180' in test_final_sweep.sh found lines 64 and 73 asserting the numeric value directly.
failures.recommendation: BA must also grep for the numeric value being changed (180) in test files and command specs, not only the constant name. For value-change tasks, both the name and old-value search patterns are required for complete scope analysis.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed FAIL verdict is appropriate. Seven findings returned: (1) My line 86 claim was partially overstated — that assertion uses bare name match and does not fail after the change; (2) same-file stale prose at agents/push-analyst.md:159 (covered by BA's Should Have but worth noting); (3) commands/push.md is an operational spec, not passive docs; (4) security assessment should be more precise about preserved-grant paths; (5) remote_url validation gap is pre-existing; (6) historical docs/dev/* references should not be updated; (7) FAIL verdict is correct but reason should be: BA scope incomplete on two active tests failing, one test too weak, and operational command spec diverging.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Corrected AC4 V3 claim (line 86 does not fail — it is the assertion that remains green but becomes a weak guard). Added security nuance about preserved-grant paths. Classified remote_url gap as pre-existing out-of-scope. Confirmed commands/push.md as operational spec, not passive docs. Refined FAIL reason accordingly.

objections.finding: BA's Won't Have ('No change to any other file') is contradicted by the existence of hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh AC4 assertions that hard-assert the value 180. Proceeding with BA's current scope will cause the regression test suite to report AC4 failures. Dev cannot follow BA's scope definition without breaking the test harness.
objections.required_action: BA must revise the affected files list to include hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh. The AC4 V1 and V2 assertions must be updated to assert 600, not 180. This is a MUST HAVE item, not optional.

objections.finding: BA's affected_files = ['agents/push-analyst.md'] is incomplete. QA verified two additional files are affected: hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh (runtime test failure) and commands/push.md (operational spec divergence).
objections.required_action: BA must update the affected files list and add acceptance criteria covering the test update and command spec update.

objections.finding: BA's scope_expansion searched only by constant name. Numeric value references (the literal '180') in test assertions and command specs were not checked. This gap in investigation method caused the scope analysis to miss the test harness dependency.
objections.required_action: For all value-change tasks, BA must search for both the constant name AND the numeric value being replaced, specifically targeting test assertion files.

passing_checks.detail: PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 at agents/push-analyst.md:164 confirmed by QA direct read. execute-push.py expiry check at line 166 confirms the failure mode. Round-trip path description is accurate.
600 is user-verbatim. Value is appropriate for the described round trip (10 minutes provides sufficient margin over the round-trip latency that caused the failures).
agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 at line 164 is correct. BA used Read tool to confirm line location. QA grep independently confirmed.

AC1 and AC2 are properly written GIVEN/WHEN/THEN with executable grep/diff checks. Codex feedback on brittle line-number assertions was applied by BA (AC1 uses content-pattern match, not absolute line number). AC form is correct for the scope BA defined.

BA's codex_consult shows all five codex findings were applied (AC1 line-number brittleness, comment wording, diff scope constraint in AC2, security wording precision, task ID supplied explicitly). The upstream BA codex consultation quality is good.

summary.verdict_reason: BA scope is incomplete. The regression test at hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh:64,73 asserts PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 exactly and will fail after the change. BA must expand scope to include this test file (must-have update) and the commands/push.md operational spec (should-have update), then resubmit for QA.

required_ba_revisions: Add hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh to Must Have scope: update AC4 V1 assertion from 180 to 600; update AC4 V2 Python AST assertion from val.value == 180 to val.value == 600; strengthen AC4 V3 to also require 600 in commands/push.md rather than just a bare name match.
Add commands/push.md to Should Have scope: update the '## Push-analyst grant TTL' section to document 600s, revise history sentence to note 180->600 increase for this task.
Update scope_expansion.all_occurrences and affected_files in context JSON to include both newly-identified files.
Update acceptance_criteria JSON to add a third AC covering the test harness update (or expand AC2 diff scope to enumerate expected line changes in test_final_sweep.sh).
Investigation method: for future value-change tasks, add a numeric-value grep step (searching for the old value '180') alongside the constant-name grep.
